title: Suffolk: Annette's Birthday Party (Wingfield)

main_route.excerpt: We slept on Annette's drive. Max in house, Kate

stops.excerpt: evening concert at Wingfield church, old re-constructed organ, near Halesworth, Amici sang

boundary_flags: Page B_0189_L is boundary_mixed: the top portion of the page belongs to a prior trip (Wroxeter visit). The Suffolk/Wingfield entry begins at the 'June 9th' heading. Places listed in the 'places' array for this page (Wroxeter, London, Cirencester, St Albans) are from the prior trip's text; Australia, Singapore, Granada are contextual mentions (where guests live/have been), not stops on this trip.
summary: A short overnight trip to Suffolk on 9 June 2012: Simon, Eve and the dog Tinder drove to Wingfield to celebrate Annette's birthday party, attended an evening concert by Amici at Wingfield church (featuring a reconstructed old organ), and slept on Annette's drive before returning home to Birmingham.
extraction_notes: Single diary page (B_0189_L), boundary-mixed with preceding trip. Year 2012 taken from trip_id; the transcription metadata recorded 'iso: 2003' for the date, which appears to be an extraction artefact. Halesworth is mentioned only as a geographic reference ('near Halesworth') and is not a separate stop. The concert venue (Wingfield church) is treated as an excursion from the overnight base at Annette's home in Wingfield rather than a distinct main-route stop, since it is part of the same evening at the same location. Travellers are explicitly named as Simon, Eve and Tinder (the dog); no children are mentioned as attending this trip.
